reviews.ownerResponse:
I confess I am disappointed and in fact quite surprised to read this review posted on March 5, 2023. I must state at the outset that I am ethically precluded from discussing publicly any conversations I have with clients. In fact, I am precluded even from identifying people who have consulted with me in a professional capacity.
That having been been said, the name of this reviewer was not immediately familiar to me. However, there was some ring of familiarity to the circumstances that she relates here. That led me on a hunt through my calendar, and I found that I did consult with a person having the same first name but a different family name, all the way back on August 4, 2022 -- a full 7 months before the posting of this review this week. Again, I am precluded from discussing the legal advice that I give to anyone who consults me in my office. However, I can confirm in the abstract the legal principle that the reviewer states here: "provisions in a [real estate] sale contract do not survive a closing unless explicitly stated." Beyond that, again in the abstract, there is no lawyer anywhere who can opine on a legal contract that the lawyer has not seen. Every real estate contract of sale contains at least one Rider, and sometimes two Riders. Without actually seeing the contract and the Riders, no real estate lawyer could know whether there is an "explicit statement" contradicting the usual principle that representations in a sale contract do not survive the closing.
So without confirming or denying that this reviewer was the same person with whom I spent an hour and a quarter in my office on August 4, 2022, following what would have been an additional initial telephone conversation to set up the appointment for any in-office meeting, I would treat any future situation in exactly the same fashion. I never would attempt to give an opinion on any contract I have not personally reviewed. Moreover, it is not the practice in my law firm to give free telephone conversations. Once more without discussing specific consultations, sometimes the lawyer's answer is not what the individual hopes to hear. For certain, I did not represent this reviewer when she purchased the subject property. So the contract she signed was not my work.
